    96	
    97	const isLeafNode = (node) => {
    98	  if (!node || typeof node !== "object") return false;
    99	  return !Array.isArray(node.children) || node.children.length === 0;
   100	};
   101	
   102	const collectLeafNodes = (node, result = []) => {
   103	  if (!node || typeof node !== "object") return result;
   104	
   105	  if (isLeafNode(node)) {
   106	    result.push(node);
   107	    return result;
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  if (Array.isArray(node.children)) {
   111	    node.children.forEach((child) => collectLeafNodes(child, result));
   112	  }
   113	
   114	  return result;
   115	};
   116	
   117	const hasOwnStatusOverall = (node) =>
   118	  !!node &&
   119	  typeof node === "object" &&
   120	  Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(node, "statusOverall");

    15	public class MatchResultProspect implements java.io.Serializable, Comparable<MatchResultProspect> {
    16	    private Annotation annotation;
    17	    private double score = 0.0d;
    18	    private String scoreType;
    19	    private MediaAsset asset;
    20	    private MatchResult matchResult;
    21	
    22	    public MatchResultProspect() {}
    23	
    24	    public MatchResultProspect(Annotation ann) {
    25	        this();
    26	        this.annotation = ann;
    27	    }
    28	
    29	    public MatchResultProspect(Annotation ann, double score, String type, MediaAsset asset) {
    30	        this();
    31	        this.annotation = ann;
    32	        this.score = score;
    33	        this.scoreType = type;
    34	        this.asset = asset;
    35	    }
    36	
    37	    /**
    38	     * Attach a PairX inspection MediaAsset to this prospect. Used by
    39	     * {@link MatchInspectionPairxEnricher} in Phase C to enrich
    40	     * prospects after the MatchResult has been persisted (empty-match-
    41	     * prospects design Track 2 C13: PairX is now non-blocking and
    42	     * runs without holding the outer Shepherd across HTTP).
    43	     */
    44	    public void setAsset(MediaAsset asset) {
    45	        this.asset = asset;
    46	    }
    47	
    48	    public MediaAsset getAsset() {
    49	        return asset;
    50	    }
    51	
    52	    public Annotation getAnnotation() {
    53	        return annotation;
    54	    }
    55	
    56	    public double getScore() {
    57	        return score;
    58	    }
    59	
    60	    public String getType() {
    61	        return scoreType;
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    public boolean isType(String type) {
    65	        if (type == null) return (this.scoreType == null);
    66	        return type.equals(this.scoreType);
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    public boolean isInProjects(Set<String> projectIds, Shepherd myShepherd) {
    70	        // if we have no projects to filter on, we consider this to be in it
    71	        if (Util.collectionIsEmptyOrNull(projectIds)) return true;
    72	        if (annotation == null) return false;
    73	        Encounter enc = annotation.findEncounter(myShepherd);
    74	        if (enc == null) return false;
    75	        return enc.isInProjects(projectIds, myShepherd);
    76	    }
    77	
    78	    public String toString() {
    79	        return scoreType + "=" + score + " on " + annotation + " for " + matchResult;
    80	    }
    81	
    82	    public JSONObject jsonForApiGet(Shepherd myShepherd) {
    83	        JSONObject rtn = new JSONObject();
    84	
    85	        rtn.put("annotation", MatchResult.annotationDetails(annotation, myShepherd));
    86	        rtn.put("score", score);
    87	        // skipping scoreType since this is currently only used filtered by scoreType already
    88	        if (asset != null) {
    89	            JSONObject aj = asset.toSimpleJSONObject();
    90	            aj.put("url", asset.webURL()); // we have no "safe" url
    91	            rtn.put("asset", aj);
    92	        }
    93	        return rtn;
    94	    }
    95	
    96	    // used in sorting
    97	    @Override public int compareTo(MatchResultProspect other) {
    98	        // we invert this so higher score is first
    99	        int comp = Double.compare(other.score, this.score);
   100	        // if the scores are the same (comp == 0), we want to ensure consistent/deterministic
   101	        // ordering (otherwise tied scores come back random order), so we use annot id
   102	        if ((comp == 0) && (this.annotation != null) && (this.annotation.getId() != null) && (other.annotation != null))
   103	            return this.annotation.getId().compareTo(other.annotation.getId());
   104	        // scores are *not* equal, so we just let comparison stand as-is
   105	        return comp;
   106	    }
   107	}

   157	
   158	    private int populateProspects(String type, JSONArray annotIds, JSONArray scores,
   159	        JSONArray externs, String externRef, Shepherd myShepherd)
   160	    throws IOException {
   161	        if ((annotIds == null) || (scores == null))
   162	            throw new IOException("null annotIds or scores");
   163	        if (annotIds.length() != scores.length())
   164	            throw new IOException("mismatch in size of annotIds/scores");
   165	        if (this.prospects == null)
   166	            this.prospects = new HashSet<MatchResultProspect>();
   167	        int num = 0;
   168	        this.numberProspects += annotIds.length(); // true number of prospects
   169	        for (int i = 0; i < annotIds.length(); i++) {
   170	            double score = scores.optDouble(i, -Double.MAX_VALUE);
   171	            String id = IBEISIA.fromFancyUUID(annotIds.optJSONObject(i));
   172	            Annotation ann = getAnnotationFromAcmId(id, myShepherd);
   173	            if (ann == null) {
   174	                System.out.println("WARNING: populateProspect failed to load annotId=" + id +
   175	                    "; skipping; score=" + score);
   176	                continue;
   177	            }
   178	            MediaAsset ma = null;
   179	            // we only try if we have a true value in externs[i]
   180	            if ((externs != null) && (externs.length() > i) && externs.optBoolean(i, false))
   181	                ma = createInspectionHeatmapAsset(externRef, id, myShepherd);
   182	            this.prospects.add(new MatchResultProspect(ann, score, type, ma));
   183	            num++;
   184	            if (num >= MAXIMUM_PROSPECTS_STORED) {
   185	                System.out.println("[DEBUG] hit max (" + MAXIMUM_PROSPECTS_STORED +
   186	                    ") number storable prospects on " + this);
   187	                break;
   188	            }
   189	        }
   190	        return num;

  1572	function score_sort(cm_dict, algo_name) {
  1573	console.warn('score_sort() cm_dict %o and algo_name %s', cm_dict, algo_name);
  1574	//.score_list vs .annot_score_list are these the same? seem to be same values
  1575		if (!cm_dict.annot_score_list || !cm_dict.dannot_uuid_list) return;
  1576		var sorta = [];
  1577	
  1578		// score_list could be either individual-scores or annotation-scores depending on the individualScores boolean global var
  1579		var score_list = {};
  1580		// to generalize this we can just make a list or func for algHasNoImageScores
  1581		var noImageScores = algHasNoImageScores(algo_name);
  1582		console.warn('score_sort() algHasNoImageScores ' + noImageScores);
  1583		if (INDIVIDUAL_SCORES || noImageScores) {
  1584			score_list = cm_dict.score_list;
  1585		}
  1586		else score_list = cm_dict.annot_score_list;
  1587	
  1588		// this tells us which annotations have illustratsions
  1589		dannot_extern_list = cm_dict.dannot_extern_list
  1590	
  1591		if (!INDIVIDUAL_SCORES && noImageScores) {
  1592			// display a message to the user that image scores are disabled for this alg
  1593			var message = "The "+algo_name+" algorithm does not return per-image match scores, so only name scores are displayed."
  1594			console.log("score_sort omitting image scores with message \"%s\"", message)
  1595			// to display this message: talk to Jon about where we should inject the algo_name into html
  1596		}
  1597	
  1598		if (score_list.length < 1) return;
  1599		//for (var i = 0 ; i < cm_dict.score_list.length ; i++) {
  1600		for (var i = 0 ; i < score_list.length ; i++) {
  1601			if (score_list[i] < 0) continue;
  1602			sorta.push(score_list[i] + ' ' + cm_dict.dannot_uuid_list[i]['__UUID__'] + ' ' + dannot_extern_list[i]);
  1603		}
  1604		sorta.sort(function(a,b) { return parseFloat(a) - parseFloat(b); }).reverse();
  1605		return sorta;
  1606	}

      2       1 S    /bin/bash -c ps -o pid,ppid,stat,command | rg 'git status|32194|git'

codex
**Findings**

Medium: the retained tests guard the `must`→`filter` query-shape regression, but they do not directly pin the reverted score call site at [Annotation.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/Annotation.java:1283). If someone later inlines `2 * osScore - 1` again, the current `AnnotationMiewIDScoreTest` shape tests and `MatchResultIndivProspectsTest` would still pass. Not a blocker, but a small pass-through regression test would be valuable if there’s an easy seam.

Low: the frontend note is slightly off. React’s [MatchProspectTable.jsx](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/frontend/src/pages/MatchResultsPage/components/MatchProspectTable.jsx:432) clamps negative scores to `0` before formatting, so stale raw-cos rows will not display negative there. They are still stale and can sort differently; regenerating remains the right call. The older JSP path also skips negative scores in [iaResults.jsp](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/webapp/iaResults.jsp:1601), if that surface is still reachable.

Low: a few comments/test names still say “best-cosine” or “own cosine” after the score-scale shift, for example [MatchResult.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/ia/MatchResult.java:210) and [MatchResultIndivProspectsTest.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/test/java/org/ecocean/ia/MatchResultIndivProspectsTest.java:77). Non-functional, but worth cleaning later to avoid reintroducing the raw-cos mental model.

**Answers**

1. Algebra checks out under the Lucene `cosinesimil` assumption: WBIA `score = (2 - distance) / 2`, with `distance = 1 - cos`, becomes `(2 - (1 - cos)) / 2 = (1 + cos) / 2`. That matches storing OpenSearch Lucene `_score` directly at [Annotation.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/Annotation.java:1283).

2. Removing `openSearchScoreToCosine` is OK for this repo. Grep shows no remaining production callers. I would only deprecate instead of remove if Wildbook is treated as a Java library consumed by external compiled code, which does not appear to be the case here.

3. The cosine math tests were correctly removed because that behavior is intentionally gone. The only meaningful lost guard is a direct “OS `_score` is persisted unchanged” test, noted above.

4. For the test deployment surface, regenerating stale rows is the right call. A blanket SQL migration is too easy to get wrong unless you can precisely identify only C17 vector-generated prospects. For production, I’d want a more explicit stale-row invalidation/migration plan.

5. Yes, explicitly tag the Stage 1 + Stage 2 coupling in commit messages and the PR description. A short “must deploy together” note is useful release engineering, not just documentation.

6. No frontend change needed for new rows. The formatter handles `[0, 1]`; just note it clamps stale negative values to `0` in React rather than showing them negative.

I did not modify files or rerun Maven tests; this was a read-only code review against the workspace diff and references.

Medium: the retained tests guard the `must`→`filter` query-shape regression, but they do not directly pin the reverted score call site at [Annotation.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/Annotation.java:1283). If someone later inlines `2 * osScore - 1` again, the current `AnnotationMiewIDScoreTest` shape tests and `MatchResultIndivProspectsTest` would still pass. Not a blocker, but a small pass-through regression test would be valuable if there’s an easy seam.

**Answers**

1. Algebra checks out under the Lucene `cosinesimil` assumption: WBIA `score = (2 - distance) / 2`, with `distance = 1 - cos`, becomes `(2 - (1 - cos)) / 2 = (1 + cos) / 2`. That matches storing OpenSearch Lucene `_score` directly at [Annotation.java](/mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2/src/main/java/org/ecocean/Annotation.java:1283).

2. Removing `openSearchScoreToCosine` is OK for this repo. Grep shows no remaining production callers. I would only deprecate instead of remove if Wildbook is treated as a Java library consumed by external compiled code, which does not appear to be the case here.

3. The cosine math tests were correctly removed because that behavior is intentionally gone. The only meaningful lost guard is a direct “OS `_score` is persisted unchanged” test, noted above.

4. For the test deployment surface, regenerating stale rows is the right call. A blanket SQL migration is too easy to get wrong unless you can precisely identify only C17 vector-generated prospects. For production, I’d want a more explicit stale-row invalidation/migration plan.

5. Yes, explicitly tag the Stage 1 + Stage 2 coupling in commit messages and the PR description. A short “must deploy together” note is useful release engineering, not just documentation.

6. No frontend change needed for new rows. The formatter handles `[0, 1]`; just note it clamps stale negative values to `0` in React rather than showing them negative.

I did not modify files or rerun Maven tests; this was a read-only code review against the workspace diff and references.
